observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:

All AOs remit/deposit all collections intact daily or at the close of each business day and for collectors assigned to the field, where travel time from their places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00;

The Liquidating Officer perform proper verification procedures as prescribed in the manual and sign only the RCD after checking that all entries are correct and properly verified with; and

The Accountable Form Custodian only release new accountable forms after ensuring that previously issued accountable forms are properly accounted for and collections from which are duly remitted to the liquidating officer.

We reiterated our recommendations to management to:

Validate the items initially identified as part of the lost properties and submit a report to COA showing the list of structures destroyed by fire on March 2016 with corresponding amounts reflected in the books of accounts; and

Include in the supporting documents satisfactory explanation or reason(s) for the delay in the submission of the notice of loss and request for relief from accountability together with the supporting documents to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that RFQs are duly filled with complete essential information as prescribed by relevant provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR to promote transparency and efficiency during the procurement process.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that all AOs render the report of accountability for accountable forms at the end of the month for consolidation by the Municipal Treasurer. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make an official directive to all AOs for the preparation of the RAAF and submission of the same to the former and to the Audit Team.

We recommended that management, on a regular basis:

Inform the Office of the Auditor within  10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and

Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We reiterated the following recommendations to management:

The GSO retain a copy of the signed POs and submit to the COA Auditor within 5 working days, irrespective of amount, together with the related supporting documents; and

The GSO furnish the COA Auditor with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of goods and services.

We recommended the following to management:

Consider adding more competent and efficient personnel in the office of the BAC Secretariat;

Submit the complete set of required documents pertaining to the above-cited projects according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and

Strictly observe the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for timely, systematic, effective review and evaluation of the project.

We recommended the following to management:

Submit to the Auditor copies of the pre-repair evaluation report and approved detailed plans showing in sufficient detail the scope of work or extent of repair of the above-mentioned repairs and replacements.

We recommended the following to management:

Justify the non-submission of the above cited contracts and its supporting documents within the prescribed period;

Submit the complete set of required documents pertaining to the above-cited projects and of all other projects implemented in CY 2019 according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and

Strictly observe the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for timely, systematic, effective review and evaluation of the project.

We recommended that all stages in the procurement process be faithfully adhered to in line with the commitment of the Government to promote good governance and its effort to adhere to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in procurement. We further recommend that appropriate charges be filed against erring Officials if warranted.

We recommended to management to observe proper and effective internal control in the disbursement of public funds and see to it that all claims against the government funds are covered with complete documentation to ascertain its legality and propriety and to ensure that the disbursements were actually incurred.

Recommendations

We recommended the following to management:

The Office of the GSO and the Inventory Committee to immediately convene and conduct full physical count of the PPE to include those PPEs owned by LGU as at December 31, 2024, and conduct full reconciliation on the accounting records with the results of the actual physical count based on the RPCPPE
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor a reconciled PPELC on all PPE accounts after the results of the reconciliation; and
The Office of the Municipal Mayor to strictly monitor the full implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE.

Recommendations

We recommended, and management agreed on the following:
The GSO and the Disposal Committee to attend training and seminars in the conduct of the disposal of government properties in line with the new issuance of the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties;
The Disposal Committee conduct of actual disposal of unserviceable properties following the guidelines and procedures in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries in the derecognition of the unserviceable properties in the books after disposal and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendations

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations, and management agreed to:
Validate the items initially identified as part of the lost properties and submit a report to COA showing the list of structures destroyed by fire in March 2016 with corresponding amounts reflected in the books of accounts; and
Include in the supporting documents satisfactory explanation or reason(s) for the delay in the submission of the notice of loss and request for relief from accountability, together with the supporting documents to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendations

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that management:
Maintain the practice of sending demand letters to employees with outstanding CAs requiring them to settle unliquidated CAs and do the same on a regular basis; and immediately furnish the audit team with copies of the demand letters duly received by the concerned accountable officers;
Initiate action to recover unsettled CAs of personnel who were already separated from service, upon failure by the latter to respond to demand letters for violation of Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code; and
Require the Accountant to monitor outstanding cash advances of every official & employee and report the status of their liquidations to the Local Chief Executive on a regular basis for appropriate action by the latter.

Recommendation

We recommended that management consider the preparation of a request for write-off of the Other Receivable account, which was considered dormant following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendations

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer endorse the reactivation of dormant accounts to the LGU’s Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the issuance of SB resolution;
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Contractor for the release of retention money; and,
The Municipal Treasurer monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent accounts from becoming dormant.

Recommendations

We recommended that management:
Initiate the request for the write-off of dormant Due to NGAs account balances in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
Make representations with identified source agencies for the return of unutilized balances and/or reversion of the same to the National Treasury, as warranted.

Recommendation

We recommended that management ensure that the Municipal Treasurer strictly monitor the remittance of collections of the Revenue Collectors and for the latter to observe the frequency of deposits/remittances of collections under their custody to avoid possible loss of funds and misstatements of cash and the corresponding income accounts.

Recommendations

We recommended, and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer to:
Secure an adequate fidelity bond for the accountable officers to safeguard the government assets in case of loss through theft, misappropriation, and other losses due to fortuitous events; and
Monitor properly the change in accountability of each accountable officer and see to it that the amount of their bond premiums conforms with the amount of their accountabilities.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Accountable Officer keep an updated cashbook which shall be made available to management and COA in the conduct of audit of cash and accounts of the accountable officer.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR by directing the BAC  to (a) require from prospective bidders the submission of (i) statement of all its ongoing government and private contracts, including contracts awarded but not yet started, if any, whether similar or not similar in nature and complexity to the contract to bid; (ii) statement of SLCC which is similar in nature to the project; construction methods, equipment utilization schedule, and COSHP for infrastructure projects.

Recommendation

We recommended that Management ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant record the repair and maintenance of DRRM Operation Center;
LDRRMO ensure that PPAs in LDRRMFIP are well planned and ready for implementation and funds optimally utilized; and,
LDRRMO conduct a regular review and evaluation of the status of implementation of the different PPAs under the LDRRMFIP.

Recommendation

We recommended that the LDRRMO submit immediately the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund for the months of October to December 2024 and henceforth submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.

Recommendation

We recommended that the LSB submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished to the local sanggunian concerned and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the timelines.

Recommendation

We recommended that the LSB ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports on the website of the LGU and/or in at least three conspicuous public places and that the same be made not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter, as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021.

Recommendations

We recommended to the GAD Focal Person the following:
Maximize the utilization of GAD Funds through employing effective and efficient measures in the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects to attain the objectives for which funds were provided; and
For those PPAs already implemented but still have unutilized amounts, consider utilizing those amounts to implement new GAD-related PPAs to ensure more benefits are derived for the intended beneficiaries.

Recommendations

We recommended, and management agreed on the following:
The concerned offices to immediately conduct reconciliation of their existing records pertaining to membership contributions and/or loan payments to ensure exact and accurate amounts for withholding and remittance;
Immediately remit to Philhealth, GSIS, and Pag-IBIG, the prior periods’ unremitted contributions of the LGU employees; and
Ensure full and timely withholding and remittance of monthly contributions in accordance with the schedule of payments prescribed by the said government corporations to avoid penalties.

We reiterated our previous recommendations to management to:

Send demand letter to the named accountable officer/beneficiary of the loan; and

Determine the nature of the loans receivable and status of collectability of this account and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We reiterated our recommendation to the management to make immediate and appropriate action on the transfer of funds for these reciprocal accounts to its appropriate account in order to present reliable information particularly on the fund balances maintained by the LGU.

We recommended the following to management:

The GSO immediately processed the renewal of registration of all motor vehicles owned by the LGU with expired registration, the penalty for the late registration be borne by the person responsible; 

The GSO process as soon as possible the transfer of vehicle registration of that one identified Honda 150 to the name of the LGU; and

The Local Chief Executive to instruct the GSO and end-users of the motor vehicles, particularly those whom said vehicles were issued, to ensure that registration of vehicles be renewed prior to its expiration, with the information that penalties incurred in case of late registration be borne by those in-charge of the said vehicles.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Accountant:

Verify the validity of all recorded payables and make confirmations to check whether said claims actually exist or not especially those which are long outstanding and consider the reversion of those payables with no actual claimant after exhausting all necessary remedies to validate the existence of such obligations;

Payments of all payables with valid claimants and complete supporting documents be immediately made or processed in accordance with the applicable rules and regulations and upon the presumption that budgets for these obligations were already earmarked through the Obligation Request duly signed by the budget officer to avoid accumulation of unpaid obligations; and

Effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify other accounts erroneously booked in the Accounts Payable account to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LDRRMO submit immediately the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund for the CY 2023 and henceforth to submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.

We recommended the following to management:

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to return any remaining balance of the unexpended LDRRMF-CO to the general fund;

The Municipal Accountant transfer only the unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF QRF/MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liability-DRRM account excluding the unexpended LDRRMF-CO; and

The Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to facilitate the immediate transfer of any unexpended balance of LDRRMF-QRF/MOOE to the Special Trust Fund books and bank account, as well as the immediate return of the LDRRMF-CO back to the general fund books and general fund bank account.

We recommended the following to management:

Coordinate with the Provincial Government of Zamboanga del Sur to discuss the herein issue and consider a possible revision of the Memorandum of Agreement to clarify the purpose of the concerned heavy equipment funded out of the LDRRM Fund of the municipality as well as its accounting treatment and ownership, and process involved in cases where the contract will be rescinded; 

Submit the minutes of meeting, resolution and any related document of the MDRRMC on the realignment/reprogramming of various PPAS funded under the unexpended LDRRMF to raise the counterpart fund with the Provincial Government of Zamboanga del Sur for the procurement of backhoe (excavator); and

Submit to the Audit team the revised MOA and any related documents immediately once the same is already available.

We recommended to Management that the  GAD Focal Point System endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children and Elderly of the SB, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313. Further, include in the ordinance the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions, b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof, and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

The Municipal Accounting Office and the RHU coordinate with IPHO Zamboanga del Sur and DOH and seek clarification for the proper recording of the received Covid Vaccines, Drugs and Medicine, and other medical supplies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of RPT and SET collected in 2022 and submit the corresponding JEV to the Audit Team. Likewise, Accounting Office to follow the correct accounting entries regarding advance payments.

We recommended the following to management:

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office submit quarterly report on uncollected fees from Market Operations to Municipal Accounting Office for purposes of recording and monitoring; and

The Municipal Accountant draw journal entries to reflect the necessary adjustments to the income and receivable accounts based on the reports received from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and MEEDO Water System Division.
We recommended that the Committee on Women and Family with the assistance of the GFPS formulate GAD Code for the LGU and submit the same to the sanggunian for enactment into an Ordinance.

We recommended that the LGU capacitate its GFPS to undertake gender analysis by sending them to training seminars and require the creation and/or maintenance of an updated GAD database containing sex disaggregated data and other gender-related information which shall serve as basis in conducting gender analysis, identifying gender issues and determining the appropriate PAPs that would address the identified gender issues to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommend that the BAC notify or invite in writing the Audit Team to attend in all procurement stages, including the post-qualification stage, in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended the following to management:

Maintain a pool of observers from all recognized private group of sector or discipline relevant to any kind of subject matter of the procurement to be bid that has the knowledge, experience and expertise in procurement;

Invite only those observers that have the knowledge, experience and expertise in procurement and belong to a recognized private group of sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand; and

Indicate in the Abstract of Bids the time, date and place of bid opening and amount of bid security.

We recommended the following to management:

Require the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all Local Roads and a Road Map;

The Municipal Accountant provide adjusting journal entries to recognize changes upon completion of the required inventory reports and reconciliation with the Registry of Public Infrastructure; and

Depreciation be provided accordingly.

Likewise, Accounting Office to follow the correct accounting entries regarding advance payments.

We recommended the following to management:

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in their respective records. Exert extra efforts to locate the missing documents to support the adjustment in the Cashbook; and

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit.

We recommended the following to management:

(Restated in Part II under Observation No. 5)

We recommended that management make immediate and appropriate action on the transfer of funds for these reciprocal accounts to its appropriate account in order to present reliable information particularly on the fund balances maintained by the LGU.

We recommended the following:

The RHU properly maintain stock cards or any monitoring tool for Drugs and Medicines, and other medical supplies;

The RHU to make proper and immediate coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accounting Office for recording of received medical items; and

The Municipal Accounting Office and the RHU coordinate with IPHO Zamboanga del Sur and DOH and seek clarification for the proper recording of the received Covid Vaccines, Drugs and Medicine, and other medical supplies.

We recommended the following to Management:

Ensure that purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method, and charge the same to expense accounts upon issuance/utilization based on appropriate inventory records or documents, such as the Request and Issue Slip (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI);

Direct the Accounting Unit to maintain perpetual inventory records, such as the Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory stock.  The subsidiary ledger cards shall contain the details of the General Ledger accounts; and

For check and balance, require the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Stock Cards (SC) for inventories for reconciliation with the ledger cards of the Accounting Unit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments for the recognition of the prior years’ depreciation and the corresponding accumulated depreciation of Other Land Improvements, taking into consideration the cost, year and month of purchase/completion, and useful life. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant provide for its annual depreciation, if applicable.

We recommended the following to management:

The MEEDO Water System Division submit quarterly report on uncollected water fees to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and to the Municipal Accounting Office for proper monitoring of income and receivables, and recording of the same in the books of the LGU;

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office submit quarterly report on uncollected fees from Market Operations to Municipal Accounting Office for purposes of recording and monitoring; and

The Municipal Accountant draw journal entries to reflect the necessary adjustments to the income and receivable accounts based on the reports received from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and MEEDO Water System Division.

We recommended the following to management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. No.1445 shall be imposed; and

Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents within the reglementary period.

We recommended that management, on a regular basis:

Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;

Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and

Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We reiterated the following recommendations to management:

Direct all concerned personnel to submit copies of POs to the GSO immediately upon perfection for control and monitoring purposes; 

The GSO retain a copy of the signed POs and submit to the COA Auditor within 5 working days, irrespective of amount, together with the related supporting documents; and

The GSO furnish the COA Auditor with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of goods and services.
We reiterated our previous recommendation that the MPMIU require the PG the submission of the Physical and Financial Report that reflects the status of project implementation as of December 31, 2022.

We recommended that:

a)	The Municipal Accountant record the adjustments for the unrecorded reconciling items;

b)	The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit; and 




c)	The Municipal Accountant verify the cause of the GF – 20% EDF bank account having a negative book balance and prepare the appropriate adjustment to correct the error.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	Send immediately demand letters to employees with outstanding cash advances and require   them to immediately settle the amount granted. If liquidation is still not achieved, consider withholding their salaries or any amount due them until balances have been fully settled; and

b)	Require the Accountant to monitor outstanding cash advances of every official & employee and report the status of their liquidations to management.

We recommend the following to management:

a)	The inventory committee complete the physical inventory count in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPE (RCPPE) duly approved by the Head of the Agency and distribute the same as follows:
		
Original	–	COA Auditor, through the Accounting Division/Unit
Copy 2	–	Accounting Division/Unit
Copy 3	–	Supply and/or Property Division/Unit
Copy 4	–	Inventory Officer/Committee

b)	The GSO and Accounting Units undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records;

c)	The GSO prepares the List of PPE found at station and Non-existing/Missing PPE using the format provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 based on the RCPPE and follow the procedures for the disposition of non-existing/ missing PPEs provided under Paragraph 7 of the Circular;

d)	The Accounting Unit take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize PPE found at station and to recognize loss of PPE and to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officer/personnel for lost properties under the latter’s accountability; and

e)	Ensure that items of PPE reflected in the agency’s FS are actually existing and if no complete information is available for the dropping of non-existent/missing properties, full disclosure must be made in the Notes to FS.
We recommended that all AOs render the report of accountability of accountable forms at the end of the month for consolidation by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	The AOs indicate the RCD number prior to submission to the Liquidating Officer. Ensure one number series for each accountable officer;

b)	The AOs completely fill up the Part C -Accountability for Accountable Forms portion of the RCD to reflect their actual accountability as of remittance date; and

c)	The Liquidating Officer make the required verification of the RCDs, particularly the checking of the statement of accountable forms and the physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector before affixing his signature in the RCD.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	Ensure that procedures for collections, remittances and reporting under the circular are followed and should there be delegation of duties, the same must be documented and put into writing; and

b)	Keep the Cash Book as one record and not as a separate monthly report for proper monitoring of the cash in treasury.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	Justify the non-submission of the above cited contracts and its supporting documents within the prescribed period;

b)	Submit the complete set of required documents pertaining to the above-cited projects and of all other projects implemented in CY 2019 according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and

observations.recommendation: require the concerned officer to submit the reportorial requirements to reflect realistic and updated timelines to avoid the undesirable effects of delayed release of succeeding tranches, late completion of the projects, feedbacks being rendered irrelevant and the project implementers being exposed to risk of reputational loss;

observations.recommendation: require the Proponent Group to submit the necessary financial reports as required in the Enterprise Investment Agreement; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to direct the MPMIU to require the Proponent Group the immediate submission of the necessary documents to show that taxes withheld from the suppliers were fully remitted to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: Moreover, we recommended that management ensure that the PG strictly comply with their obligation particularly on withholding of taxes and its subsequent remittance to the BIR by closely monitoring the activities of the PG and providing timely assistance to the latter in accordance with the IMA and EIA.

observations.recommendation: immediately submit all accounting and project reports, if any, with complete supporting documents to facilitate post-audit of accounts and transactions to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension, as applicable; and

observations.recommendation: strictly observe the reglementary period in the submission thereof in the succeeding accounting period in compliance with existing rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption be submitted to the Auditor’s Office so that the used of the LGU’s motor vehicle can be properly monitored/controlled and regulated as required in the regulations and to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.reason: This audit observation has been reiterated and included in Part II, par. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal DRRM Officer coordinates with the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: This audit recommendation has been reiterated and included in Part II, par. 22.4(b) of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all stages in the procurement process be faithfully adhered to in line with the commitment of the Government to promote good governance and its effort to adhere to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in procurement. We further recommend that appropriate charges be filed against erring Officials if warranted.
observations.reason: Management still failed to conduct the bidding process meticulously in accordance to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 because there is still an audit observations anchored on the same issue which was discussed properly in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management to observe proper and effective internal control in the disbursement of public funds and see to it that all claims against the government funds are covered with complete documentation to ascertain its legality and propriety and to ensure that the disbursements were actually incurred.
observations.reason: This audit recommendation still not complied with because for current year’s audit, there are still issues concerning incomplete submission of the supporting documents. This is due to management’s laxity in the execution of the internal control.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officials responsible for the preparation and issuance of POs submit the same within the prescribed period and notify the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours after deliveries of procured items to enable the Auditor to conduct timely inspections of the same.
observations.reason: Management was inconsistent in the implementation of this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Require the collectors to turn over their collections daily and regularly to the Municipal Treasurer to preclude the misuse of cash in their possession thereby ensuring the availability of cash for the smooth operations of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management still continue this practice despite repetitive advice. Some collectors comply, but some are still not remitting on time.

observations.recommendation: The Liquidating Officer must ascertain that all accountable forms issued to each collector must be reflected in the Report of Collection and Deposit in each and every liquidation to be made.
observations.reason: Only the accountable forms brought by the accountable officer during the liquidation were included in Record of Collections and Deposits.

observations.recommendation: Also, the collections shall be reported regularly as income to come up with a fair presentation of the financial statements and accurate financial condition and results of operations of the Municipality for a given period of time.
observations.reason: Although this was the intention of the management, but there is a log of time to submit and process the transaction given that the office of the Municipal Accountant does not cater for that transaction only, there are some other transactions aside from collections.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should require his Liquidating Officer to remit the collections daily as soon as remittance has been received from various collectors together with her own collections.  This must be turned-over to the Municipal Treasurer intact so he can deposit the same to the bank on the next banking day.
observations.reason: Despite repetitive reminders as to this audit recommendation, management still continue to practice this kind of control in handling collections.

observations.recommendation: We hereby recommend, the property Officer concern should furnish the auditor said delivery documents as prescribed so as to ensure transparency and proper accountability, and to ascertain that paid goods has been actually delivered and services been rendered.
observations.reason: Management is inconsistent in the implementation of this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. re-organize the Inventory Committee and require it to conduct the actual
physical count of the Municipalitys property, and prepare and submit the
RPCPPE and RPCRNS to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each
year, including the IIRUP; and
b. constantly monitor the status of physical inventory taking to ensure its
completion and submission of complete inventory reports within the deadline.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team the copy of PAR
corresponding the purchases of PPE during CY 2019 for review;
b. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all PPE counted by the Inventory
Committee are issued with PAR and provided with property card which should
be regularly updated;
c. Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify the PPE from the records and
DVs of prior years, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer for
infrastructure assets;
d. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Schedule of
PPE and Schedule of Accumulated Depreciation corresponding to the
balances in the general ledger for review;
e. Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the regulation on depreciation; and
f. Municipal Accountant to maintain the PPELC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. make the necessary adjustment in the books by debiting the affected PPE
accounts in the total amount of P17,003,685.14 and crediting the Due to NGAs
and Prior Period Adjustment by P7,033,185.56 and P9,970,499.58,
respectively, for fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements; and
b. ensure that the purchased PPE charged against the funds received from NGAs
which are already under the control of the Municipality and its economic
benefits flow to the latter are recorded as asset and revenue.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist, to plan
and adopt collection strategies to recover the loaned funds from the borrowers
such as offering payment alternatives adjusted to fit the borrowers financial
capacity and continuous monitoring and follow-up; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to
establish the correct balance of Other Receivables account.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to maintain a supplementary record that will facilitate the monitoring of individual
loan balance and preparation of supporting schedule at any given time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:













































(Note: The original recommendations in the report immediately follow the observation and include actions for the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer, Appraisal Committee/Team and Municipal Accountant to locate documents, facilitate transfer of tax declarations, facilitate titling, furnish copies of titles, properly appraise donated lots, and record parcels based on CY 2018 revised tax declaration. The precise verbatim recommendation paragraphs were included in the main report after the observation.)

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
facilitate the submission of a new request for write-off and the relevant supporting
documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for evaluation of the Audit
Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Chairman to submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit
Team for further evaluation of paid procurement transactions;
b. BAC Chairman to ensure strict compliance with the documentary requirements
to substantiate the procurement activities of the Municipality;
c. Municipal Engineer to ensure that the program of works for the projects that
will be constructed by phase are in compliance with Section 3 i) of Annex A of
the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
d. Municipal Accountant to require the concerned contractors/suppliers to refund
the amount of liquidated damages totaling P22,092.08;
e. Municipal Engineer to closely monitor/supervise the actual accomplishments
of Municipal projects to ensure that they are performed and completed as
scheduled; and
f. Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidated damages in future
transactions, if any, are duly recognized and deducted before payments are
made to contractors/suppliers on the account of its procurement activities; and
g. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the DV is supported with proper and
complete documents before processing payment.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate compliance with the
IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding the period of action on procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:











(Note: The original recommendations: instruct the Municipal Accountant to inform the Municipal Agriculturist regarding unutilized funds; if to be implemented, instruct Municipal Agriculturist to facilitate implementation and monitor; and instruct Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to process disbursement documents for return of unutilized funds and remittance of shares to NT. These recommendations appear verbatim in the report following the observation.)

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned implementing offices to:
a. submit to the Audit Team at the beginning of every year the list of all on-going
government PPAs of the immediately preceding year and list of PPAs for
implementation during the current year;
b. inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the project or before
the start of the program/activity if the appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices have been posted, with supporting picture/s showing the posted
project signboard/public notice at the project site, to facilitate validation; and
c. submit to the Audit Team the monthly monitoring report on the status of
implementation of each PPA.

We recommended that the LGU’s accounting unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee to conduct complete inventory count of all property, plant and equipment at least once a year in accordance with regulations.

We recommended that management observe proper disclosures in the Notes to FS, particularly on reconciliation of cash and cash equivalents figure per SCF with that in the SFPos, reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus (deficit), and reconciliation of budget and actual amounts with that per SFP.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits the collections regularly and intact to avoid misuse of government funds, pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS-LGU. Also, the Municipal Accountant provide the details of the Cash – Local Treasury account balance to the auditor for reconciliation with the established amount of shortage for adjustment/booking of shortage in the book of accounts.

We recommended that management, on regular basis:
Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We recommended that concerned personnel submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor’s Office regularly for timely verification on the reasonableness of the agency’s fuel consumption to prevent possible wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that Management:
ensure that purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, shall be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method, and charge the same to expense accounts based on appropriate inventory records or documents, such as the Request and Issue Slip (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI);
direct the Accounting Unit to maintain perpetual inventory records, such as the Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory stock.  The subsidiary ledger cards shall contain the details of the General Ledger accounts; and
for check and balance, require the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Stock Cards (SC) for inventories.  The balance in quantity per SC should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the Accounting Unit.

We recommended that the BAC submit a written explanation justifying their decision in declaring LR Enterprises as the LCRB despite an expired Business Permit and henceforth to conduct the bidding process meticulously and be observant to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that management:
Require the former municipal mayor to submit request for relief from accountability and its supporting documents as prescribed under COA Circular 92-751; and
Include in the supporting documents satisfactory explanation or reason(s) for the delay of submission of the notice of loss and request for relief from accountability together with the supporting documents to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that management require the Sangguniang Bayan Members and OIC-Budget Officer to submit a written explanation justifying the charges for the purchase of motor vehicles to GAD fund. Also, the GAD Focal Person to establish a GAD Database/ Sex-disaggregated Data in order to identify the gender issues prevailing in the Municipality to facilitate the appropriateness of expenditures to GAD fund.

We recommended the following:
The PRDP I-Reap Sub-Project, particularly on the transfer of funds to the PG, should be implemented strictly in accordance with the EA and I-Reap Manual, as well as to the relevant provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001;
The MPMIU should immediately secure from PG the lacking documents and submit the same to the Audit Team; and
The MLGU must ensure that compliance to the fundamental principles for all financial transactions and operations of the government as laid down in Section 4 of PD 1445 will be strictly followed.

We recommended that Management require the Project Accountant and concerned personnel to:
resolve the recurring delays noted and to strictly observe the reglementary period in the submission of subsequent accounting and project reports in compliance with existing rules and regulations; and
immediately submit all accounting and project reports with complete supporting documents to facilitate post-audit of accounts and transactions.

We recommended that management:
Justify the non-submission of the above cited contracts and its supporting documents within the prescribed period;
Submit the complete set of required documents pertaining to the above-cited projects and of all other projects implemented in CY 2019 according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and
Strictly observed the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for timely, systematic and effective review and evaluation of the project.

We recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and
Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.

We recommended the following:
submit documents evidencing that the resource persons and facilitators were authorized or accredited by the GPPB-TSO;
provide a detailed training design showing the topics given and the number of hours for each topic and the resource persons/lecturers and facilitators assigned therewith; and
lastly, submit a separate document showing a detailed computation of the honorarium per resource person/lecturer and facilitator based on actual hours rendered following the computation set forth in Budget Circular 2007-1.

We recommended that management:
Charge the project contractor liquidated damages for the unperformed portion for every day of delay;
Demand the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible; and
Institute legal action against the contractor if warranted.

We recommended that management:
Send the LGU GFPS to attend seminars and trainings related to GAD to update their knowledge and build their competencies in the execution of GAD related programs and activities; and
Require the LGU GFPS to conduct HGDG test in evaluating the agency’s PAPs gender sensitivity and responsiveness for client focused activities and GMEF for organization focused activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Project Management Implementation Unit (MPMIU), to:
Closely monitor the implementation of the project at the site in order that proper assistance will be timely provided to the farmer-beneficiaries; and
Require the PG to submit the necessary documents immediately upon full implementation of the 2nd tranche activities particularly the physical and financial accomplishment reports and its supporting documents to avoid further delay in the liquidation of the funds received especially that the project implementation is close to completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entry for the closed SDA account and henceforth, ensure that the BRS is prepared monthly for all current accounts of the Municipality, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: made and BRS is being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to work together in the implementation of the planned projects and activities for 20 percent DF to ensure timely implementation so that the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes are fully achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer-Designate, in coordination with the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board, expedite the: a. preparation of the revised 10-Year SWMP for submission and approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission; and b. construction of the Materials Recovery Facility and titling of the proposed lot for sanitary landfill to ensure that the programs and activities for solid waste management are based on the NSWMF which aims to protect the public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the revised 10-year SWMP.
observations.reason: The Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) was constructed at Barangay Pacatin Ilaya. The proposed sanitary landfill in Barangay Pacatin Ilaya is not yet ready for construction as the titling of the lot is still in-process. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 12, Page 71.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format as provided in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and the LDRRMO to submit the said report to the COA Auditor for monitoring and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required reports for January to April 2019.
observations.reason: Other required reports for May to December 2019 were not submitted. Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 11, Page 68.

observations.recommendation: (c)	require the Accounting Department to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards/subsidiary ledger cards; and

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed presentation of discussion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as provided in COA Circular 2012-002 and to make necessary corrections and/or adjustments as the case maybe on the mathematical errors observed in the “Balance” column.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions provided under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure that charges out of the 20% Economic Development Fund should be one of those allowable development projects.

Moreover, make representation with the officials of the association to discuss matters pertaining to the possible recovery of the transferred fund and possible services and assistance that the MLGU may provide to the association and lieu of the same.
We recommended that management refrain from paying out any claim from contractors or suppliers which is not expressed, specified and embodied in the contract.
Also, we request them to justify and submit a written explanation and reason behind the issuance of Notice of Award to a bidder with invalid Tax Clearance.
Lastly, explain how the LGU-Pitogo delivered the large quantity of fuel despite the lack of Fuel Depot or storage facility to justify the existence and reasonableness of the transaction.

We recommend that the LSB refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for those that are part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead, it should allocate SEF for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development.
Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth in RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We recommended that other basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the LGC and 3.0 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 should also be considered in allocating funds of SEF so as not to deprive the constituents of the benefits from the said funds.

We recommended that management exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information and discuss in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to ensure full disclosure.

It is recommended that management exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the conduct of procurement procedures and observe proper, effective and efficient advertising of Invitation to Bid that conveys relevant and necessary information to prospective bidder.

Require the collectors to limit the performance of their functions within the prescribed duties and responsibilities.  The disbursement function is not within their responsibilities hence, they could not be allowed to pay any disbursement voucher that may be presented to them for payment.  Also, the Municipal Treasurer shall see to it that the frequency of turn-over of collection shall be made daily and regularly and all accountable forms are complete as issued to them to preclude the accumulation of huge cash in the possession of the collectors thereby minimizing the possibility of misappropriating government funds.

The concerned officials must exert extra effort to prepare the required procurement plan using the PPMP per vehicles and equipment identifying the details of the parts needed in compliance with the requirement of the budget process per Step 2.3 of UBOM and 7.2 of IRR-A of RA 9184.

Also, all Purchase Request relating to the procurement of spare parts must give identity to a specific vehicle or equipment to which it will be utilized and correspondingly the waste material reports must likewise mention the same.

The BAC should prepare a procurement monitoring report to be submitted to the Municipal Mayor for approval and for submission to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester to monitor the proper implementation by procuring entities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• re-organize the Inventory Committee and require it to conduct the physical
count of properties and submit the RPCPPE, including the RPCRNS, not later
than January 31 of each year;
• require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify the properties from
prior year’s records, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer for
infrastructure assets, and provide depreciation based on its recorded cost or
revalued amount; and
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and
updated PPELC/WOABSLC and property cards, and reconcile their records
regularly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the adjusting entry for the closed SDA account and henceforth, ensure
that BRS is prepared monthly for all current accounts of the Municipality, in
compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
submit the relevant documents required underCOA Circular No. 2016-005 for
evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDC Members to prepare
and submit the IIRUP as of December 31, 2018 together with the required
supporting documents and complete the disposal of properties by providing the
needed manpower, in compliance with Section 79 of P.D.No. 1445 and Section
125, Chapter 7 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
 Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the MAO, to plan and adopt
collection strategies to recover the loaned funds from the delinquent
borrowers such as sending collection notices or payment reminders to all
borrowers on a regular basis, offering payment alternatives adjusted to fit the
borrowers’ financial capacity and continuous monitoring and follow-up; and
 Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to
establish the correct balance of Other Receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period or at the
end of the year in accordance with the above-cited Circular.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor to facilitate the titling of the 16 parcels of land to avoid contingent
claims that may arise in the future.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to submit the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be
implemented during the year at the beginning of the year; and inform the Audit
Team that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were posted
within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of
the program/activity.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to observe utmost diligence in the performance of her duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information that may affect the users in making sound judgments and economic decisions and see to it that the financial information provided in the financial statements are free from material misstatements in order to enhance the degree of confidence of intended users in the Financial Statement.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed presentation of discussion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as provided in COA Circular 2012-002 and to make necessary corrections and/or adjustments as the case maybe on the mathematical errors observed in the “Balance” column. Lastly, the management is advised to exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information that may affect the users in making sound judgments and economic decisions.

We recommend that management adhere strictly to the provisions provided under DILG-DBM JMC  No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to ensure that charges out of the 20% Economic Development Fund should be one of those allowable development projects. Moreover, make representation with the officials of the association to discuss matters pertaining to the possible recovery of the transferred fund and possible services and assistance that the MLGU may provide to the association and lieu of the same.

We recommend that management submit the complete set of required documents of the said project, as well as all other procurements, according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easier referencing.
Moreover, we recommend that management observe the prescribed reglementary period in submission of the required documents of all future procurements for timely, necessary, systematic and effective review and evaluation of the project.
We recommend that management refrain from paying out any claim from contractors or suppliers which is not expressed, specified and embodied in the contract.

We recommend to the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct the bidding process meticulously and be observant to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184. Also, we request them to justify and submit a written explanation and reason behind the issuance of Notice of Award to a bidder with invalid Tax Clearance.

We recommend to management, particularly the Municipal Project Management Implementation Unit (MPMIU), to closely monitor the implementation of the project at the site in order that proper assistance will be timely provided to the farmer-beneficiaries and to ensure good harvest of seaweeds especially that the implementation was delayed and untimely which greatly affect the number of kilos harvested by the farmers.

We recommend to management that the PRDP I-Reap Sub-Project, particularly on the transfer of funds to the Proponent Group, should be implemented strictly in accordance with the IMA, EA and I-Reap Manual. We recommend further that management require the MPMIU to immediately secure the lacking documents and submit the same to the Audit Team. Additionally, management must ensure that compliance to the fundamental principles for all financial transactions and operations of the government as laid down in Section 4 of PD 1445 will be strictly followed.

We recommend to management to direct the MPMIU to require the Proponent Group the immediate submission of the necessary documents to show that taxes withheld from the suppliers were fully remitted to the BIR. Moreover, we recommended that management ensure that the PG strictly comply with their obligation particularly on withholding of taxes and its subsequent remittance to the BIR by closely monitoring the activities of the PG and providing timely assistance to the latter in accordance with the IMA and EIA.

We recommended Management to require the Project Accountant and concerned personnel to:
resolve the recurring delays in the submission of accounting and project reports which affected the timely submission of annual Project Audit Report as required by the World Bank and;
immediately submit all accounting and project reports, if any, with complete supporting documents to facilitate post-audit of accounts and transactions to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension, as applicable; and
strictly observe the reglementary period in the submission thereof in the succeeding accounting period in compliance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption be submitted to the Auditor’s Office so that the used of the LGU’s motor vehicle can be properly monitored/controlled and regulated as required in the regulations and to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period. Lastly, explain how the LGU-Pitogo delivered the large quantity of fuel despite the lack of Fuel Depot or storage facility to justify the existence and reasonableness of the transaction.

We recommend that Municipal DRRM Officer coordinates with the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the LSB refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for those that are part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead, it should allocate SEF for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth in RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We recommend that other basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the LGC and 3.0 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 should also be considered in allocating funds of SEF so as not to deprive the constituents of the benefits from the said funds.

We recommend to management the following:
strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team.
require the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and (iii) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues; and
establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01

We recommend that the LSB refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for those that are part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead, it should allocate SEF for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth in RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We recommend that management should adhere strictly to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payment1s for government transactions be made by check and payable directly to the creditor or thru a cash advance granted to the disbursing officer for a specific purpose. Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials who earlier paid the transaction in cash. Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash advance issued to them.
We further recommend that procurement of supplies and construction materials should be governed by R.A. 9184 known as “Government Procurement Reform Act”.

We recommend that the officials responsible for the preparation and issuance of POs submit the same within the prescribed period and notify the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours after deliveries of procured items to enable the Auditor to conduct timely inspections of the same.

We recommend management to allocate and appropriate funds for the effective implementation of Ecological Solid Waste Management Program in accordance with RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.
We recommend further that the Municipal Waste Management Board shall convert its open dumpsite into a controlled dumpsite in accordance with the guidelines set in Section 41 of the Act.

We recommend that management exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information and discuss in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.
We recommend that management create/strengthen GFPS or similar GAD mechanism to catalyze and accelerate gender mainstreaming within the agency.
